# Beatpoly — llms.txt
# https://beatpoly.com

## What Beatpoly Is
Beatpoly is a prediction-market intelligence platform. We score prediction markets for resolution risk, liquidity quality, cross-venue equivalence, and abnormal information flow. The goal is to help professional teams know which prediction-market prices are trustworthy, distorted, or unsafe to use.

Tagline: "Know which prediction markets to trust."
Previous tagline: "Stop guessing. Start harvesting." (retired — retail positioning)

## Core Product
The Beatpoly Reliability Engine scores every monitored market across four dimensions:
1. Resolution Risk — does the market resolve cleanly?
2. Liquidity & Execution Quality — does the edge survive fees, spread, and slippage?
3. Cross-Venue Equivalence — are two similar markets actually the same bet?
4. Market Integrity & Abnormal Flow — is the market showing suspicious information movement?

Output: A–F reliability rating + machine-readable scores + evidence trail via REST API.

## Who Beatpoly Serves
- Funds and prop shops: execution-aware signal evaluation
- Compliance and risk teams: insider-flow detection, audit trail
- Media and research desks: citation-readiness assessment
- Data platforms: reliability metadata for enrichment

## Enterprise API
Base URL: https://api.beatpoly.com/v1
Authentication: Bearer token
Status: Pilot access — scoped per customer
Key endpoints:
  GET /v1/markets/{id}/reliability
  GET /v1/markets/{id}/resolution-risk
  GET /v1/markets/{id}/liquidity-quality
  GET /v1/markets/{id}/cross-venue
  GET /v1/alerts/abnormal-flow
  POST /v1/execution/simulate

## Research Library (free, public)
The Learn library explains prediction-market mechanics: resolution criteria, execution friction, oracle risk, cross-venue differences, behavioral biases, and why similar markets can resolve differently.

## Key Site Pages
- / — Enterprise homepage
- /platform.html — The reliability engine (4 dimensions)
- /api.html — API reference and access request
- /use-cases.html — Funds, compliance, media, data platforms
- /methodology.html — Scoring logic and statistics audit
- /about.html — Company, beliefs, operating principles
- /learn/ — Research library (free, public)

## Venues Covered
Kalshi (CFTC-regulated, USD settlement) and Polymarket (offshore, USDC on Polygon). Coverage expanding.

## Operating Principles
1. Use public information only
2. Document every material claim
3. Separate market reliability from trade recommendation
4. Flag uncertainty rather than hide it
5. Do not encourage manipulation of outcomes or data sources

## Not Investment Advice
Beatpoly provides market reliability intelligence. Reliability scores do not predict event outcomes and do not constitute investment advice.

## Affiliate Disclosure
Beatpoly uses affiliate links to Kalshi and Polymarket in its research library. This does not affect reliability scoring or editorial decisions.
